image_metadata.File:Commons-logo.svg: The Wikimedia Commons logo, SVG version.
image_metadata.File:Commutative diagram illustrating pr: Diagram designed to illustrate how the Fourier transform may be used to solve a problem when it is simplified by applying the Fourier transform.
image_metadata.File:CQT-piano-chord.png:
The result of a[constant-Q transform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/constant-Q_transform)applied to the waveform of a C major chord played on a piano. The x axis corresponds to frequency mapped to the standard pitch names \(increasing from lower pitches on the left to higher frequencies on the right\).  The y axis is time, with the start of the piano chord happening at the bottom and the release happening at the top \(approximately 8 seconds later\)
The darker areas correspond to higher values of the constant Q transform. The peak values are vibrating frequencies of the strings. The leftmost 3 strongest peaks are at the fundamental frequencies of the notes of the C major chord \(C, E, G\). The the remaining peaks to the right of the fundamental frequencies are[overtones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/overtone).

Note that Brown-Puckette algorithm on the showcqt uses kernels derived directly from frequency domain, the bandwidth calculation is actually[variable-Q](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Variable-Q_transform), and showcqt uses linear/nth root amplitude scaling rather than logarithmic.
image_metadata.File:Fourier transform of oscillating fu: This image shows the magnitude plot of the Fourier transform of a function, marked up with information specific to other images in the same sequence.
image_metadata.File:Fourier transform time and frequenc:
A visualization of the relationship between the time domain and the frequency domain of a function, based on its Fourier transform.
The Fourier transform takes an input function f \(in red\) in the "time domain" and converts it into a new function f-hat \(in blue\) in the "frequency domain".


In other words, the original function can be thought of as being "amplitude given time", and the Fourier transform of the function is "amplitude given frequency".

Shown here, a simple 6-component approximation of the square wave is decomposed \(exactly, for simplicity\) into 6 sine waves. These component frequencies show as very sharp peaks in the frequency domain of the function, shown as the blue graph.
image_metadata.File:Fourier unit pulse.svg: svg version of the Fourier transforms of the unit pulse function
image_metadata.File:Offfreq i2p.svg: This image shows the real and imaginary parts of an oscillating function times the complex exponential oscillating at a different rate.
image_metadata.File:Onfreq.png: This image shows the real and imaginary parts of an oscillating function times the complex exponential with the same oscillation.
image_metadata.File:OOjs UI icon edit-ltr-progressive.s: An icon from the OOjs UI MediaWiki lib.
image_metadata.File:Phase shift.svg: Two sinusoidal waves offset from each other by a phase shift$\theta$.
image_metadata.File:Sinc function (normalized).svg:
[Graph](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Graph_of_a_function)of the normalized[sinc function](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/sinc_function)sin\(πx\)/\(πx\).
[Mathworld's version](http://mathworld.wolfram.com/SincFunction.html). The original was generated by gnuplot, while the more recent one was generated with matplotlib. File size was reduced by efficient coding of the axis drawing and labeling and by a reworking of the graph's path. The resulting path is smoother even though fewer points are used in its determination.

## omegatron's instructions

.mw-parser-output .see-also{background:var\(--background-color-interactive-subtle,\#f8f9fa\);margin:1em 0;border:1px solid var\(--border-color-subtle,\#c8ccd1\);padding:2px;color:var\(--color-base,\#202122\);clear:left;overflow:hidden}![File:System-search.svg](../../archives/Wikimedia%20Commons/System-search.svg)

See also:[Wikipedia graph-making tips](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:How_to_create_graphs_for_Wikipedia_articles).Then I opened the resulting SVG file in[Inkscape](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inkscape), changed the line colors, brought the plot lines to the top, made all the lines 3 px wide, made the grid lines 2px wide and 50% transparency.
